records.ety: [F. rception, L. receptio, fr. recipere, receptum. See Receive.]

defs: The act of receiving; receipt; admission; as, the reception of food into the stomach; the reception of a letter; the reception of sensation or ideas; reception of evidence.
The act or manner of receiving, especially of receiving visitors; entertainment; hence, an occasion or ceremony of receiving guests; as, a hearty reception; an elaborate reception.
quotes: What reception a poem may find.

defs: Acceptance, as of an opinion or doctrine.
quotes: Philosophers who have quitted the popular doctrines of their countries have fallen into as extravagant opinions as even common reception countenanced.
